content.full_text:
March 17, 1992

Ms. Blanca Breeze
Attorney at Law
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms. Breeze:

I am one of the Directors of Santa Clara Valley Water District, which is a Special District formed by the legislative act. I was first elected to this position in November, 1982, and have served in this capacity since January, 1983. Prior to that, I was an engineer for the same District for the period of 13-1/2 years. Before taking the oath of office, I requested to the District Board that I may be granted "leave without pay" from my employment for the duration that I served as a Director. But, based upon the opinion of the District Counsel then, the Board denied my request. I assumed the Office of the Director, but refused to resign because I strongly believed that I was eligible for the leave as requested. The matter rested there for about eight years.

In 1990, there were two similar cases with other special districts which were tried in the court. In both cases, the court ruled that the employees had the right to retain their employee status while sitting on the Board. Based upon these rulings, I requested our Board to reconsider their decision and grant me the "leave without pay" as I had requested previously. The Board reviewed both cases and agreed that I should have been granted the leave. They are willing to do so now. But our present District Counsel is concerned about conflict of interest issues that might arise if Board approves leave without pay for me, who is one of the Board members.

One alternative that is considered to avoid the conflict of interest issue is adoption of a resolution by the District Board that any past or present employee, who is elected to serve on the District Board, will be granted "leave without pay" status for the duration that he/she is the Board Director and will be reinstated as a full time employee in the same or equivalent position when he/she ceases to be the Director. I have enclosed the relevant correspondence and the briefs of the two cases for your information and file.

I would greatly appreciate if the FPPC would provide me with its opinion on the validity of resolving the conflict of interest issue in this manner. Thank you for your assistance.

Yours truly,

JOE PANDIT
Director, District One

Enclosures

The image is a photograph of a handwritten letter with blue ink, which appears to be written in English. The text is mostly illegible due to the quality and style of handwriting, but some parts are partially visible or partially obscured by the blue ink. Here is a summary of what can be partially read:

- The letter is addressed to "Mr. Bruce".
- It mentions the Office of the Director, but refers to action because I strongly believe that as a Director, that based upon the opinion of the District Council that the Board meeting will be held on the 13-15 June, before making the only decision, I should review for the guidance that I received from the Board of the District since January 1987. Prior to that, I was an employee for the same District serving in the teaching service. I was not elected to this position in November 1987, and have I am one of the Directors of Swiss Club Area North District, which is a Special District.
- There is also mention of the Secretary of State's Office, but the rest of the content is mostly illegible.

If you need further analysis or transcriptions, please provide higher quality scans or images.
